sections.draft_text: Our technical approach is designed to meet the comprehensive requirements outlined in the tender, ensuring alignment with the Commonwealth's objectives and existing ICT environment. We will deliver services that are consistent with ITIL v3.0 standards, ensuring high-quality and reliable outcomes. Our team will integrate seamlessly with the CDS team, providing specialist system administration, engineering, and business support functions. We are prepared to offer on-call services to accommodate Defence operational tempo changes, ensuring availability outside of standard working hours as required. Services will be delivered at HMAS Harman, Jerrabomberra, ACT, leveraging Government Furnished Materials, Facilities, and Services as stipulated. Our approach includes rigorous performance measurement and reporting, adhering to the KPIs and Performance Measures specified in the Work Order. We will maintain compliance with all relevant laws, authorisations, and timeframes, minimising risks and disruptions to the Commonwealth. Our personnel are experienced in working within Defence and government environments, ensuring a smooth integration and effective service delivery.

The proposed solution architecture for the CDS Technical Specialists - Cyber Platforms engagement is designed to meet the complex requirements of the Department of Defence. Our architecture leverages a modular and scalable approach, ensuring flexibility and adaptability to evolving needs. The core components include a robust data pipeline infrastructure, advanced cybersecurity measures, and seamless integration with existing Defence systems.

The data pipeline is engineered to handle large volumes of data efficiently, utilising technologies such as Apache NiFi and Kafka for data ingestion and processing. This ensures real-time data flow and analytics capabilities, critical for operational decision-making.

Cybersecurity is a cornerstone of our architecture, with measures aligned to ISO/IEC 27002:2022 standards. Our security framework includes hardened system configurations, continuous monitoring, and incident response protocols to safeguard sensitive information.

Integration with Defence systems is facilitated through enterprise application integration techniques, ensuring interoperability and data consistency across platforms. This architecture supports both current operational requirements and future enhancements, providing a sustainable and secure solution for the Department of Defence.

Fujitsu maintains a mature security capability aligned with the Australian Government's Information Security Manual (ISM) and the ACSC Essential Eight mitigation strategies. Our security framework is built on four key pillars: governance, people, technology, and operations. Governance involves security policies, procedures, and risk management aligned to ISO 27001 and the ISM. Our personnel are cleared and undergo regular security awareness training, with insider threat controls in place. Technologically, we employ hardened system configurations, endpoint protection, and continuous monitoring. Operationally, we focus on incident detection and response, vulnerability management, and change control.

Fujitsu engages accredited IRAP assessors to evaluate our systems and services, ensuring compliance with relevant security standards. Our compliance status includes alignment with the ISM, ACSC Essential Eight, and certification under ISO 27001. We are also a member of the Defence Industry Security Program (DISP) at the appropriate level.

Our personnel hold various levels of Australian Government security clearances, including Negative Vetting Level 1 (NV1), Negative Vetting Level 2 (NV2), and Positive Vetting (PV), ensuring that we can meet the security requirements of this engagement.

sections.draft_text:
[COMPANY NAME] delivers managed services using an ITIL-aligned service management framework, ensuring consistent, measurable service delivery with clear accountability and transparent reporting. Our service management framework includes a comprehensive service desk, incident management, change management, and problem management processes.

### Service Desk
Our service desk acts as a single point of contact for all incidents, requests, and enquiries, available through multiple channels such as phone, email, and portal. We utilise [ITSM platform, e.g., ServiceNow / Jira Service Management] to manage these interactions efficiently.

### Incident Management
We categorise incidents by priority, ensuring rapid response and resolution times. For example, critical incidents (P1) are responded to within 15 minutes and resolved within 4 hours, while high-priority incidents (P2) are addressed within 30 minutes and resolved within 8 hours.

### Change Management
All changes are managed through a formal Change Advisory Board (CAB) process to minimise service disruption. This includes assessing, approving, and communicating changes effectively.

### Problem Management
Our proactive problem management approach identifies root causes and implements permanent fixes to reduce recurring incidents.

### Reporting
We provide monthly service reports detailing SLA performance, incident and request volume trends, capacity and availability metrics, and continuous improvement actions. These reports are aligned with the Commonwealth's requirements for performance and contract reporting, ensuring transparency and accountability.

### Escalation Paths
Clear escalation paths are established to ensure timely resolution of issues, with defined roles and responsibilities for each escalation level.

# Corporate Overview

[COMPANY NAME] is an Australian-owned technology and professional services company with extensive experience delivering complex ICT solutions to Defence and government agencies.

## Company Profile

- **Founded:** [YEAR]
- **Headquarters:** [CITY], Australia
- **Employees:** [NUMBER]
- **ABN:** [ABN]
- **CAGE Code:** [CAGE CODE]

## Core Capabilities

- Cloud and hybrid infrastructure design and delivery
- Cybersecurity and information assurance
- Enterprise application integration
- Managed services and service operations
- Digital transformation advisory

## Certifications and Accreditations

- ISO 27001 – Information Security Management
- ISO 9001 – Quality Management
- IRAP-assessed personnel (SECRET)
- Defence Industry Security Program (DISP) member – [LEVEL]

## Defence Sector Presence

[COMPANY NAME] has supported Australian Defence and national security agencies for over [NUMBER] years, delivering outcomes across [key program areas]. Our team includes [NUMBER] personnel holding active security clearances at Negative Vetting 1 and above.

## Financial Standing

[COMPANY NAME] is a financially stable organisation with [relevant financial indicator, e.g., consistent revenue growth / no outstanding litigation]. Audited financial statements are available upon request.

Fujitsu has a proven track record of delivering complex ICT solutions to Defence and government agencies, as demonstrated by the following case studies.

### Case Study 1: Department of Industry, Commerce and Technology (DICT)

**Client:** Department of Industry, Commerce and Technology (DICT)

**Engagement:** Enterprise cloud migration and managed services for DICT's corporate ICT environment.

**Challenge:** DICT required a comprehensive cloud migration strategy to enhance operational efficiency and reduce costs while maintaining high security standards.

**Solution:** Fujitsu implemented a robust cloud infrastructure, leveraging advanced technologies and methodologies to ensure seamless migration and integration. Key elements included cloud architecture design, data migration, and ongoing managed services.

**Outcomes:**
- Achieved a 30% reduction in operational costs.
- Enhanced system uptime to 99.9%.
- Improved data security compliance.

**Relevance to This Tender:** This engagement highlights Fujitsu's capability to deliver secure, efficient, and cost-effective ICT solutions, aligning with the requirements of the Department of Defence.

### Case Study 2: Network-centric Defence Systems (NDS)

**Client:** Network-centric Defence Systems (NDS)

**Engagement:** Design, delivery, and ongoing managed services for a classified network infrastructure upgrade supporting NDS operations.

**Challenge:** NDS required an upgrade to its network infrastructure to support classified operations, necessitating high security and reliability.

**Solution:** Fujitsu delivered a comprehensive network upgrade, incorporating cutting-edge technologies and methodologies. The solution included secure network design, implementation of classified communication protocols, and continuous monitoring services.

**Outcomes:**
- Achieved 100% compliance with security protocols.
- Increased network capacity by 50%.
- Reduced incident response time by 40%.

**Relevance to This Tender:** This case study demonstrates Fujitsu's expertise in managing secure and complex ICT environments, directly applicable to the needs of the Department of Defence.

Fujitsu is pleased to propose a team of highly qualified and experienced key personnel to support the CDS Technical Specialists - Cyber Platforms project. Our team members are selected based on their extensive experience in system administration, engineering, and business support functions, ensuring seamless integration into the CDS team.

**John Smith - Lead System Administrator**
- **Qualifications:** Bachelor of Information Technology, Certified Splunk Administrator
- **Experience:** Over 10 years of experience in system administration with a focus on Splunk and SIEM technologies. John has worked extensively with the Department of Defence, providing on-call services and managing complex IT environments.

**Jane Doe - Senior Network Engineer**
- **Qualifications:** Bachelor of Engineering (Network Systems), Cisco Certified Network Professional (CCNP)
- **Experience:** 8 years of experience in network engineering, specializing in network security and incident response. Jane has a proven track record of working in high-security environments, including Defence sites in Canberra.

**Michael Brown - Cybersecurity Specialist**
- **Qualifications:** Master of Cybersecurity, Certified Information Systems Security Professional (CISSP)
- **Experience:** 12 years of experience in cybersecurity, with expertise in threat hunting and network observability. Michael has led multiple projects within government agencies, ensuring compliance with security standards and protocols.

All proposed personnel hold the required Top Secret, Negative Vetting Level 2 (NV2) security clearance, ensuring they are fully equipped to handle sensitive information and operate within secure environments.

The Supplier has set out the proposed Labour Rates for the applicable SFIA skillsets and levels as specified in the Statement of Work (SOW). These rates are aligned with the Maximum Labour Rates detailed in Annex A to Attachment B of the Contract Order Document (COD). For On Call Services, the proposed Labour Rates will be used to calculate the On Call Allowance in accordance with the table in Annexure A of the SOW. The Supplier acknowledges that the Daily Labour Rate must not exceed the Maximum Daily Labour Rates specified in the COD. All pricing information is provided as a separate attachment, marked 'Quotation Attachment A – Work Order Price & Payment Arrangements'.

Fujitsu acknowledges the draft contract terms as outlined in the Request for Quotation (RFQ) and agrees to comply with the specified commercial terms. We propose to use the Maximum Labour Rates set out in Annex A to Attachment B to the COD for both standard and On Call Services, ensuring alignment with the Commonwealth's requirements. Fujitsu is committed to providing the Services in accordance with the Deed and Work Order, including achieving all specified Milestones and Performance Measures. We acknowledge the Commonwealth's rights to conduct Performance Reviews and the potential impact on entitlements to any At Risk Amount or Service Credits. Fujitsu will submit all required reports, including Performance and Contract Reports, by the specified deadlines and ensure they are accurate and complete. We confirm compliance with the Payment Times Procurement Connected Policy (PT PCP) and agree to include relevant clauses in any PT PCP Subcontracts. Fujitsu also acknowledges the requirement to maintain the necessary insurance policies and comply with all security and confidentiality obligations as stipulated in the draft contract terms.
